q: What data can a Genie Space query and how does Unity Catalog govern access to it?
a: A Genie Space queries tables and views registered in Unity Catalog — not raw cloud storage files, unregistered Parquet files, or external sources that haven't been surfaced through a UC schema. You scope a Space to up to 30 datasets; each must be a UC table or view the space author has SELECT on. Access control works in two layers: the warehouse uses the author's embedded compute credentials to establish the session, but every SQL query Genie generates executes under the asking user's own UC grants, row filters, and column masks. A user cannot coax Genie into returning rows or columns they aren't entitled to — UC enforces permissions at query time exactly as it would for a hand-written query. The practical implication for data engineers: column masking and row-level security on UC tables carries through Genie automatically, so PII is protected without any Genie-specific configuration. The wrong assumption is that Genie has privileged access — it has exactly the caller's access.
fj: Genie can query any file in my data lake as long as I point it to the right S3 path.
fs: Genie only queries tables and views registered in Unity Catalog. Raw cloud storage files aren't queryable. Access is enforced per-user via UC grants, row filters, and column masks — not a single privileged service account.
